heading: Run a Case-Sensitive Partial Match Query
body:
autocomplete

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that begin with `haw`.

```json
{
  "autocomplete": {
    "query": "haw",
    "path": "plot"
  }
}
```

phrase

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains the phrase `new york` with a maximum distance of 5 between terms.

```json
{
  "phrase": {
    "query": "new york",
    "path": "plot",
    "slop": 5
  }
}
```

regex

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that match the regular expression pattern.

```json
{
  "regex": {
    "query": "(?i)new.*york",
    "path": "plot"
  }
}
```

wildcard

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that match the wildcard pattern.

```json
{
  "wildcard": {
    "query": "new*york",
    "path": "plot"
  }
}
```

MongoDB Compass displays the search results in the pipeline results panel.

This tutorial describes how to create an index on the
`sample_mflix.movies` collection and run partial string queries
against the `plot` field using mongosh in a local deployment.

To return matches for partial string queries, you can use one of the following operators:

- The [autocomplete](autocomplete-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for a word or phrase that contains the sequence of
  characters that you specify with your query.

- The [Phrase](phrase-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for documents that contain the terms in your query
  string at the distance you specify between the terms.

- The [regex](regex-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for strings using regular expression.

- The [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields using special characters in your query to match any
  character.

You can also use the [text](text-ref) operator with a custom analyzer
for more fine-grained control over partial matching. To learn more, see
[Custom analyzers](custom-analyzers).

This tutorial takes you through the following steps:

- Set up a MongoDB Search index on the `plot` field in the
  `sample_mflix.movies` collection.

- Run MongoDB Search query for a partial string against the `plot` field in
  the `sample_mflix.movies` collection using
  [autocomplete](autocomplete-ref), [Phrase](phrase-ref), [regex](regex-ref), and
  [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operators.

Before you begin, ensure that your cluster meets the following requirements.

To complete the tutorials, you must have:

- An Atlas cluster with MongoDB version 6.0 or higher or a MongoDB
  self-managed cluster with MongoDB version 8.2 or higher.

- The [Load Sample Data Into Atlas](sample-data) loaded into your
  cluster.

- [Project Data Access Admin](<Project Data Access Admin>) or higher access to your project to create MongoDB Search indexes.

- [Atlas UI](fts-tutorial-run-query), [mongosh](mongodb-shell), [Compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass), or a supported
  [MongoDB Driver](drivers) to run queries on your cluster.

> **Note:** You can run MongoDB Vector Search queries by using any driver
> through the $vectorSearch aggregation stage.
> These tutorials include examples for a selection
> of clients. Refer to the specific tutorial page for details.
>
> You can also complete these tutorials with local deployments that you create
> with the Atlas CLI or with an on-prem deployment. To learn more, see
> [Create a Local Atlas Deployment](atlas/cli/current/atlas-cli-deploy-local) and
> [Self-Managed Deployments](manual/self-managed-deployments).

To learn more about how to implement partial matches, see [MongoDB Text Search: Substring Pattern Matching Including Regex and Wildcard, Use Search Instead](https://medium.com/mongodb/mongodb-text-search-substring-pattern-matching-including-regex-and-wildcard-use-search-instead-3633c6f7e604).
